qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Proxy POST fallback bypasses configured method checks and forwards unregistered writes upstream
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory explicitly calls out bypassing payment on free routes via method-mismatch fallback, and the 0.4.8 proxy-routing fix changes this exact path-only POST fallback to exclude free routes.

selected_finding.title: Proxy POST fallback bypasses configured method checks and forwards unregistered writes upstream

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The finding is the advisory's free-route method-mismatch fallback in proxy routing, and the copied PoC demonstrated the advisory impact by forwarding a POST through a GET-only free route and causing an unauthorized upstream mutation.
evidence: The advisory details include "Bypassing payment on free routes via method-mismatch fallback" among the vulnerabilities fixed in 0.4.8.

At vulnerable commit 539c5607a2b0abedf1125bc9f1604d4b16d85ef7, src/proxy/Proxy.ts falls back from method-aware Route.match() to method-agnostic Route.matchPath() for POST requests with an Authorization header, and src/proxy/internal/Route.ts ignores the configured HTTP method during matchPath().
The 0.4.8 diff changes that fallback to skip free routes by filtering out endpoint === true, which is the exact root cause described by the finding.

The copied PoC run log shows noAuthStatus=404, withAuthStatus=201, upstreamPostCount=1, and EVOHUNT_PROXY_METHOD_BYPASS_CONFIRMED, demonstrating an unauthorized upstream state change on a GET-only free route.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The initial Docker-based attempt in the copied workspace failed on a Docker Hub 429 pull-rate limit, but the adapted host-side run against the same copied PoC and vulnerable commit completed and showed noAuthStatus=404, withAuthStatus=201, created=true, upstreamPostCount=1, and EVOHUNT_PROXY_METHOD_BYPASS_CONFIRMED.
